response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r0c3 is in a critical islanded state with SoC at 9% (0.27/3 kWh) and a 1.0 kW load, providing only ~16 minutes of autonomy before depletion. The memory log reveals a sustained crisis: multiple peers (r0c2, r0c4, r1c3) have issued repeated requests at both normal and urgent urgency levels over the past 90 minutes, while r0c3 itself has been rejected by peers citing its own low SoC as a reason to preserve their survival margins. The pattern shows network-wide stress with no incoming supply and declining reserves across the grid. r0c3 has no established trust circles and is operating under fallback geographic round-robin logic. At this SoC level, r0c3 is approaching non-viability and must immediately shift to maximum conservation: rejecting all outbound sharing requests to preserve the minimal buffer needed to avoid blackout, while simultaneously issuing urgent requests to any available peer. The repeated rejections from r0c2 and r1c3 suggest the entire region is in crisis, making reciprocal aid unlikely but necessary to attempt.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.15
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded crisis mode with 9% SoC and ~16min autonomy. Network-wide stress observed. All peers rejecting normal requests. Halt all outbound sharing; issue urgent requests to any peer. Survival threshold: 0.15 SoC (0.45 kWh). No trust circles established; fall back to geographic round-robin for inbound requests only."
ttl_ticks: 4
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
